task.title: geometry_cache_metrics migration + N+1 fix
task.why: No way to see which SKUs are hot vs cold → can't optimize pre-warming. Also: ProductService.get_variant() iterates all products and all variants on every call (O(P×V)) — must use a cached lookup dict.

step.code:
"""geometry cache metrics
Revision ID: 20260502_0002 Revises: 20260429_0001 Create Date: 2026-05-02 """ from alembic import op import sqlalchemy as sa
revision = "20260502_0002" down_revision = "20260429_0001" branch_labels = None depends_on = None
def upgrade() -> None: op.create_table( "geometry_cache_metrics", sa.Column("params_hash", sa.String(96), nullable=False), sa.Column("product_id", sa.String(128), nullable=False), sa.Column("lod", sa.String(10), nullable=False), sa.Column("file_size_bytes", sa.Integer(), nullable=True), sa.Column("generation_ms", sa.Integer(), nullable=True), sa.Column("access_count", sa.Integer(), nullable=False, server_default="1"), sa.Column("last_accessed", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.text("NOW()")), sa.Column("created_at", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=False, server_default=sa.text("NOW()")), sa.PrimaryKeyConstraint("params_hash"), ) # CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY is not supported in Alembic transactions # so we use standard CREATE INDEX (table is empty at migration time) op.create_index("ix_geom_metrics_product_id", "geometry_cache_metrics", ["product_id"]) op.create_index("ix_geom_metrics_last_accessed", "geometry_cache_metrics", ["last_accessed"])
def downgrade() -> None: op.drop_index("ix_geom_metrics_last_accessed", "geometry_cache_metrics") op.drop_index("ix_geom_metrics_product_id", "geometry_cache_metrics") op.drop_table("geometry_cache_metrics")

implementation.step:
Append GeometryCacheMetrics model:
# Add import at top if not present: from datetime import UTC, datetime
class GeometryCacheMetrics(Base): __tablename__ = "geometry_cache_metrics"
params_hash: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(96), primary_key=True) product_id: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(128), index=True, nullable=False) lod: Mapped[str] = mapped_column(String(10), nullable=False) file_size_bytes: Mapped[int | None] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=True) generation_ms: Mapped[int | None] = mapped_column(Integer, nullable=True) access_count: Mapped[int] = mapped_column(Integer, default=1, nullable=False) last_accessed: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column( DateTime(timezone=True), default=lambda: datetime.now(UTC), nullable=False, ) created_at: Mapped[datetime] = mapped_column( DateTime(timezone=True), default=lambda: datetime.now(UTC), nullable=False, )

step.code:
"""Repository for geometry cache metrics — tracks hot SKUs.""" from datetime import UTC, datetime
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
from app.db.models import GeometryCacheMetrics
class GeometryCacheMetricsRepository: def __init__(self, session: Session) -> None: self.session = session
def upsert( self, *, params_hash: str, product_id: str, lod: str, file_size_bytes: int | None = None, generation_ms: int | None = None, ) -> None: """Upsert access record. Safe to call fire-and-forget.""" existing = self.session.get(GeometryCacheMetrics, params_hash) if existing: existing.access_count += 1 existing.last_accessed = datetime.now(UTC) if file_size_bytes is not None: existing.file_size_bytes = file_size_bytes else: self.session.add( GeometryCacheMetrics( params_hash=params_hash, product_id=product_id, lod=lod, file_size_bytes=file_size_bytes, generation_ms=generation_ms, ) )
def top_skus(self, limit: int = 50) -> list[GeometryCacheMetrics]: """Return most-accessed geometries — for pre-warming strategy.""" from sqlalchemy import select, desc stmt = ( select(GeometryCacheMetrics) .order_by(desc(GeometryCacheMetrics.access_count)) .limit(limit) ) return list(self.session.execute(stmt).scalars())

task.commit_message: feat(T09): geometry_cache_metrics migration + O(1) variant lookup

task.title: Prometheus metrics format at /metrics
task.why: GET /metrics returns a raw Python dict — not parseable by Prometheus. Grafana/Prometheus scraper expects text/plain with # HELP and # TYPE lines. Also missing: cache hit/miss counters critical for tuning pre-warm strategy.

implementation.step:
Update metrics state initialization to include cache counters:
# Replace existing metrics dict: app.state.metrics = { # Request counters "cad_platform_requests_total": 0, "cad_platform_request_latency_ms_total": 0, # Job counters "cad_platform_jobs_queued_total": 0, "cad_platform_jobs_completed_total": 0, "cad_platform_jobs_failed_total": 0, # Cache counters (NEW) "cad_platform_cache_hits_total": 0, "cad_platform_cache_misses_total": 0, # Generation counters (NEW) "cad_platform_generate_inline_total": 0, "cad_platform_generate_inline_ms_total": 0, }

In ModelResolver.resolve(), increment counters at each decision point. Requires injecting `request.app.state.metrics` or using a global counter. Simplest approach: pass metrics dict as optional parameter.
# In routes_geometry.py, pass metrics to resolver: # resolved = model_resolver.resolve(request_obj, metrics=request.app.state.metrics)
# In model_resolver.py ModelResolver.resolve(): def resolve(self, request: ModelResolveRequest, metrics: dict | None = None) -> ModelResolveResponse: m = metrics or {}
# After cache.get() returns a hit: m["cad_platform_cache_hits_total"] = m.get("cad_platform_cache_hits_total", 0) + 1
# After all cache/db misses, before generate: m["cad_platform_cache_misses_total"] = m.get("cad_platform_cache_misses_total", 0) + 1
# After inline generate: m["cad_platform_generate_inline_total"] = m.get("cad_platform_generate_inline_total", 0) + 1
# After job queued: m["cad_platform_jobs_queued_total"] = m.get("cad_platform_jobs_queued_total", 0) + 1

tests.code:
def test_metrics_prometheus_format(): r = client.get("/metrics") assert r.status_code == 200 assert r.headers["content-type"].startswith("text/plain") body = r.text assert "# HELP cad_platform_requests_total" in body assert "# TYPE cad_platform_requests_total counter" in body assert "# HELP cad_platform_cache_hits_total" in body assert "# HELP cad_platform_cache_misses_total" in body
def test_metrics_increments_on_geometry_request(): # Trigger a geometry request client.get("/api/v1/geometry/variant/hex-bolt-iso4014-m8x30") r = client.get("/metrics") body = r.text # cache_hits OR cache_misses must be non-zero hits = int([l for l in body.splitlines() if l.startswith("cad_platform_cache_hits_total ")][0].split()[-1]) misses = int([l for l in body.splitlines() if l.startswith("cad_platform_cache_misses_total ")][0].split()[-1]) assert hits + misses > 0

task.commit_message: feat(T10): Prometheus text-format /metrics with cache hit/miss counters

task.title: Pre-warmer — Redis verify + top_skus.production.json
task.why: pregenerate_top_skus.py queues jobs without checking Redis is reachable. If Redis is down, jobs silently disappear. Also needs production SKU list.

implementation.step:
Add --verify-redis flag and verbose progress output:
# At start of main(): if args.verify_redis: try: import redis as redis_lib r = redis_lib.Redis.from_url(settings.redis_url or "redis://localhost:6379") r.ping() print(f"✓ Redis connected: {settings.redis_url}") except Exception as exc: print(f"✗ Redis not reachable: {exc}") print(" Fix REDIS_URL or run without --verify-redis for local dev.") sys.exit(1)
# Progress output per job: print(f" [{i+1}/{len(rows)}] Queued {job.job_id[:50]}... status={job.status}")
# Summary at end: print(f"\n{'─'*60}") print(f"✓ {len(jobs)} jobs queued on queue '{args.queue or QUEUE_BATCH_PREGENERATE}'") print(f" Run workers: celery -A app.workers.tasks:celery_app worker -Q batch_pregenerate --loglevel=info")

All most common mecsu.vn SKUs — sorted by sales volume estimate:
[ {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":8, "L":30,"P":1.25,"k":5.3,"s":13,"b":22,"lod":"low"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":8, "L":30,"P":1.25,"k":5.3,"s":13,"b":22,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":8, "L":40,"P":1.25,"k":5.3,"s":13,"b":28,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":8, "L":50,"P":1.25,"k":5.3,"s":13,"b":28,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":10,"L":30,"P":1.5, "k":6.4,"s":17,"b":26,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":10,"L":40,"P":1.5, "k":6.4,"s":17,"b":32,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":10,"L":50,"P":1.5, "k":6.4,"s":17,"b":32,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":12,"L":40,"P":1.75,"k":7.5,"s":19,"b":30,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":12,"L":50,"P":1.75,"k":7.5,"s":19,"b":36,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":12,"L":60,"P":1.75,"k":7.5,"s":19,"b":36,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":16,"L":50,"P":2.0, "k":10, "s":24,"b":38,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-bolt-iso4014","params":{"d":16,"L":60,"P":2.0, "k":10, "s":24,"b":44,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-nut-iso4032","params":{"d":8, "s":13.0,"m":6.8,"lod":"low"}}, {"product_id":"hex-nut-iso4032","params":{"d":8, "s":13.0,"m":6.8,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-nut-iso4032","params":{"d":10,"s":17.0,"m":8.4,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-nut-iso4032","params":{"d":12,"s":19.0,"m":10.8,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"hex-nut-iso4032","params":{"d":16,"s":24.0,"m":14.8,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"washer-iso7089", "params":{"OD":16,"ID":8.4,"h":1.6,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"washer-iso7089", "params":{"OD":20,"ID":10.5,"h":2.0,"lod":"medium"}}, {"product_id":"washer-iso7089", "params":{"OD":24,"ID":13.0,"h":2.5,"lod":"medium"}} ]

task.commit_message: feat(T11): pre-warmer Redis verify + production SKU list
